blocks.content: Nathan called the next morning at a time that suggested he'd been waiting for a reasonable hour and had landed on the very earliest one that qualified.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Okay. Tell me.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Good morning to you too.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: I've known you for six years. You sent me 'yeah it's going well' three days ago. That is not a normal response from you. That's the response of someone who doesn't know how good a thing is yet and is trying not to jinx it. I know the difference.
Ethan moved to the window. Outside, the street was still damp from last night's rain.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You're reading a lot into four words.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: I read people for a living. Well — not exactly for a living. But I'm good at it. And I haven't heard you sound like this in a while, so.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Like what.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Present. You sound present. What's her name, what does she do, and when do I get to check the math?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Check the math.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: You. Her. Whether it adds up. I'm your friend. This is a service I provide.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You're not checking any math.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Ethan.
His voice shifted slightly, losing the tease.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: You sound like a person who has decided to be present for something instead of standing half outside of it and reserving the right to leave. That's not a small thing for you. So I just want to see it. One coffee. I'll behave.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You won't behave.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: I'll be pretty good.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Pretty good isn't behaving.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: It's better, actually. Behaving is performative. Pretty good means I actually mean it.
There was a grin somewhere in his voice that Ethan could hear despite himself.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Ask her. I just want to meet the person who figured out the lights in those rooms. That's it. I promise.
A pause.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'll ask her.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: That's all I'm asking.
